repository.name: ot-tag-registry
repository.description: [DEMO] Industrial sensor tags are usually a mess of confusing spreadsheets, causing mixed-up names and super slow setups. The OT Tag Registry fixes this by giving you one central hub to easily create, organize, and standardize all your tags, keeping everything totally consistent and way easier to manage everywhere.

commit.message:
ci(windows): cache LLVM and use chocolatey for faster builds

- Add actions/cache for LLVM directory (skip download on cache hit)
- Use chocolatey instead of manual installer download
- Remove verbose debug logging
- Reduce timeout from 25min to 15min

First build: ~4min (choco install + cache save)
Subsequent builds: ~2-3min (cache hit)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(services): improve agent prompt for tag name generation

Use short codes instead of display names in tag name construction.
Add tool-failure fallback mappings for site/line resolution.
Always copy unit/datatype from search results unless the query
explicitly describes a different measurement.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Split _get_ai_client into _get_ai_client (account-level endpoint

fix(infra): add retry logic for agent setup in postprovision

commit.message:
Release v0.17.7

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Release v0.17.6

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: Add cross-platform Windows MSVC compatibility

- Fix interpreter.cpp to use platform wrappers:
  - Replace close() with tulpar_socket_close()
  - Replace usleep() with tulpar_thread_sleep()
  - Replace pthread_* with tulpar_thread_* functions
  - Make thread_entry_point cross-platform compatible

- Fix runtime_bindings.cpp:
  - Include platform_sockets.h instead of Unix headers
  - Use cross-platform socket macros

- Update build.yml:
  - Download LLVM from GitHub releases instead of Chocolatey
  - Chocolatey LLVM package lacks development headers

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: Change value_create_string parameter to const char* for MSVC

MSVC is stricter about string literal to char* conversions.
Changed function signature from 'char *val' to 'const char *val'
to allow passing string literals directly.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: Add cross-platform MSVC compatibility for Windows build

Platform Header (src/common/platform.h):
- Added TULPAR_THREAD_LOCAL macro (MSVC: __declspec(thread), GCC: __thread)
- Added TULPAR_NOINLINE macro
- Added tulpar_clock_ms() for high-resolution timing
- Added tulpar_sleep_ms() for cross-platform sleep
- Added tulpar_time_unix() for Unix timestamps
- Added sys/time.h include for Unix platforms

Runtime Bindings (src/vm/runtime_bindings.cpp):
- Replaced __attribute__((always_inline)) with TULPAR_INLINE
- Replaced __thread with TULPAR_TLS (using TULPAR_THREAD_LOCAL)
- Replaced gettimeofday() with tulpar_clock_ms()
- Removed Unix-only sys/time.h include

Interpreter Header (src/interpreter/interpreter.hpp):
- Replaced Unix socket/thread headers with platform wrappers
- Use platform_sockets.h and platform_threads.h
- Cross-platform SOCKET, THREAD_HANDLE, MUTEX_HANDLE definitions

VM (src/vm/vm.cpp):
- Replaced clock_gettime() with tulpar_clock_ms()
- Replaced usleep() with tulpar_sleep_ms()
- Replaced close() with tulpar_socket_close()
- Added platform.h and platform_threads.h includes

Compiler (src/vm/compiler.cpp):
- Converted C99/C++20 designated initializers to C++17 compatible syntax
- Changed {.field = value} to separate assignment statements

CMakeLists.txt:
- Added ws2_32 linking for socket support on Windows

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: Improve LLVM detection with -DLLVM_DIR parameter support

FindLLVM.cmake improvements:
- First check LLVM_DIR CMake variable before searching paths
- Better indentation and structure
- Removed unused EXISTS_RESULT variable
- Added proper endif() for the LLVM_DIR check

GitHub Actions workflow improvements:
- Pass -DLLVM_DIR parameter directly to cmake command
- Add debug output to show LLVM directory contents
- Show include directory contents for debugging
- Count .lib files in lib directory
- Help diagnose installation issues

This ensures CMake receives LLVM_DIR via both -D parameter and
environment variable for maximum compatibility.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: Add custom FindLLVM.cmake module for Windows builds

Created a custom FindLLVM.cmake module to handle LLVM installations
on Windows that don't include LLVMConfig.cmake (like Chocolatey package).

Changes:
- Added cmake/FindLLVM.cmake: Custom module to find LLVM on Windows
  * Searches for LLVM in standard Windows locations
  * Parses llvm-config.h to determine version
  * Provides llvm_map_components_to_libnames() function
  * Maps component names to .lib files on Windows

- Updated CMakeLists.txt:
  * Add cmake directory to CMAKE_MODULE_PATH
  * Try CONFIG mode first, fallback to MODULE mode (FindLLVM.cmake)
  * Conditional add_definitions for LLVM_DEFINITIONS

- Updated GitHub Actions workflow:
  * Set LLVM_DIR environment variable for CMake
  * Removed explicit -DLLVM_DIR from cmake command
  * Let CMake use FindLLVM.cmake module automatically

This allows building on Windows with Chocolatey LLVM package that
lacks proper CMake configuration files.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Release v0.17.4

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: Improve LLVM detection and Windows MSVC compatibility

GitHub Actions Workflow:
- Remove llvm-config.exe check (not included in Windows LLVM package)
- Add fallback to standard LLVM cmake paths
- Use LLVM root as last resort fallback
- Better directory structure logging
- More resilient to missing LLVMConfig.cmake

CMakeLists.txt:
- Try CONFIG mode first, fallback to MODULE mode
- Add manual LLVM path detection for Windows
- Enhanced LLVM status logging (DIR, INCLUDE_DIRS, LIBRARY_DIRS)
- Add link_directories for LLVM_LIBRARY_DIRS
- Link against version.lib on Windows (required by LLVM)

These changes make the build process more resilient when LLVM is
installed via Chocolatey which may not include all config files.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: address hook bugs #86, #87, #88 (#149)

- #86: Block --no-verify on rebase/merge/cherry-pick/am
- #87: Generate unique session IDs instead of hardcoded 'hook_session'  
- #88: Content-hash gating prevents redundant AGENTS.md writes
- 8 new tests, 185 total pass

Closes #86, #87, #88

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: Add native MSVC support and fix Windows CI LLVM installation

Changes to CMakeLists.txt:
- Add MSVC-specific compiler flags (/W3, /EHsc, /permissive-)
- Disable common MSVC warnings for LLVM compatibility
- Enable /bigobj flag for large object files required by LLVM
- Add separate Debug/Release configurations for MSVC
- Update platform support comment (Windows now natively supported)

Changes to GitHub Actions:
- Force uninstall existing LLVM before installing version 18.1.8
- Dynamic search for LLVMConfig.cmake location
- Store cmake directory in GITHUB_ENV for build step
- Improved error diagnostics and verification

This enables building TulparLang with MSVC on Windows without WSL.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: Use Chocolatey for LLVM installation on Windows CI

The previous method of downloading LLVM-18.1.8-win64.exe installer
did not include CMake configuration files (LLVMConfig.cmake) required
by find_package(LLVM REQUIRED CONFIG).

Changes:
- Replace manual LLVM installer download with Chocolatey package
- Add verification step to confirm LLVMConfig.cmake exists
- Enhanced error handling with detailed diagnostics

This ensures Windows builds have all necessary LLVM development files.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- restore observability/flow-logging work item to blocked status
- use lib.attrByPath directly for router recovery assertions

commit.message:
Add native Windows support with MSVC

- Created cross-platform abstraction layer (platform_*.h headers)
  - Winsock2/BSD sockets abstraction
  - Windows threads/pthread abstraction
  - LoadLibrary/dlopen abstraction

- Updated source code for Windows compatibility
  - vm.cpp: Winsock2 integration and initialization
  - runtime_bindings.cpp: Windows dynamic loading
  - jit_memory.cpp: VirtualAlloc for JIT on Windows

- Enhanced CMake build system
  - Added Windows/MSVC configuration
  - MSVC compiler flags and Winsock2 linking
  - Runtime library Windows support

- Created Windows build scripts
  - build.bat: Batch script for easy building
  - build.ps1: PowerShell script with modern UX

- Added GitHub Actions Windows CI
  - build-windows job with LLVM 18 + VS 2022
  - Automated Windows builds and releases

- Updated documentation
  - README.md: Windows as fully supported platform
  - PLATFORM_SUPPORT.md: Complete Windows guide
  - WINDOWS_TESTING.md: Testing procedures
  - WINDOWS_IMPLEMENTATION_SUMMARY.md: Full details

Windows users can now build natively without WSL!

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: convert tempo sessions to recovery when NFI is red and fatigued

The tempo case in generateWorkout() was missing the red-NFI recovery
guard present on all other session types. When NFI is red with negative
TSB (genuine fatigue), tempo sessions now correctly return a recovery
workout instead of a scaled tempo session.

Fixes the failing test: 'generates a recovery workout when NFI is red
and fatigued'

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: SilverSprint
repository.description: SilverSprint is a "Neural-First" training platform. It operates on the principle that for aging sprinters, Central Nervous System (CNS) recovery is the primary bottleneck for performance. The application must automate the detection of neural fatigue and provide actionable "Go/No-Go" training recommendations.

commit.message:
fix: resolve ImportError caused by incompatible agent framework versions

- Upgrade azure-ai-agentserver-agentframework pin from ==1.0.0b12
  to >=1.0.0b17 so pip resolves compatible transitive dependencies
  (agent-framework-azure-ai 1.0.0rc3, azure-ai-projects 2.0.0b4).
  The old b12 pin pulled agent-framework-azure-ai 1.0.0b260107 which
  imports PromptAgentDefinitionText from azure-ai-projects — a name
  that no longer exists in the GA 2.0.x release.
- Rename ChatMessage -> Message in tools.py (import + 2 call sites).
  The class was renamed in agent-framework >= 1.0.0b17 / rc3.
- Rename create_agent() -> as_agent() in main.py.
  The method was renamed in AzureAIAgentClient in the same release.

Fixes #19. All 137 tests pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: use SprintTrainingPlan.buildContext() for mock training plan

Replaced hand-crafted mockTrainingPlan (plan: []) with a call to
SprintTrainingPlan.buildContext(), which populates the full 12-week
plan array. The empty plan[] caused TrainingPlanPanel to crash on
context.plan[planWeek - 1] access, resulting in a blank demo page.

- Default agent config at ~/QuickAI/agents/coder.json

commit.message:
Render markdown tables in agent mode output (#405)

`markdown_to_text` already parsed tables via `Options::all()` but
silently dropped all table events into `_ => {}`. This adds proper table
rendering with box-drawing characters, bold headers, column-width
normalization, and alignment markers.

## Changes

- **`TableAccum` struct** — buffers column alignments, header cells,
body rows, and the in-progress cell during streaming parse
- **`render_table()` helper** — two-pass render: first computes
per-column widths, then emits header / separator / body `Line`s with
`│`/`─`/`┼` box-drawing borders and `:` alignment markers
- **Parser loop** — routes all 8 table `Tag`/`TagEnd` events;
`Text`/`Code` events inside a table cell go to the cell buffer instead
of `current_spans`
- **3 new tests** — basic two-column table, alignment markers,
empty-body table

Example rendered output for an AI-generated table:
```
│ Command │ Description       │
├─────────┼───────────────────┤
│ ls      │ List files        │
│ pwd     │ Print working dir │
```

<!-- START COPILOT CODING AGENT SUFFIX -->



<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

> there some options in the pulldown cmark to parse tables.
> try and enable it and create a test case in agent mode.rs
> the goal is to nicely format tables when the ai outputs it as markdown
text.
> make a pr if you make progress.


</details>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: HalFrgrd <4559349+HalFrgrd@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add macOS build targets and installer support (#401)

`cross` cannot build for Apple targets — it relies on Docker containers
which cannot include the Apple SDK due to licensing. macOS builds
require native runners. `install.sh` also hard-rejected Darwin.

## Release pipeline (`release.yml`)
- Add `build-release-macos` job on `macos-latest` runners building
`x86_64-apple-darwin` and `aarch64-apple-darwin` via plain `cargo build`
- Archive `libflyline.dylib` (cdylib on macOS, not `.so`)
- Remove dead commented-out apple-darwin lines from the Linux `cross`
matrix
- `publish-release` now waits on both `build-release` and
`build-release-macos`

## Installer (`install.sh`)
- `detect_os`: return `"darwin"` instead of erroring
- Set target triple to `<arch>-apple-darwin`; use `libflyline.dylib` as
the library name; skip Linux-only libc detection
- Fall back to `~/.bash_profile` on macOS when `~/.bashrc` is absent
- `verify_sha256` helper: `sha256sum` on Linux, `shasum -a 256` on macOS
(compatible checksum format)
- `sed_inplace` helper: `sed -i ''` (BSD) vs `sed -i` (GNU) — removes
the GNU-only `readlink -f` dependency

<!-- START COPILOT CODING AGENT SUFFIX -->



<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

> try and get this to build for macos using cross.
> I already use cross. search the codebase for where.
> 
> try and resolve any problems. if there are serious problems and you
don't see a way forward, report your findings and exit.
> 
> if you do succeed: think about how to deal with cross platform home
folders, libflyline.so might need to beceom
libflylibe.so/libflyline.whateverextesnionmacuses.
> 


</details>

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: HalFrgrd <4559349+HalFrgrd@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: flyline
repository.description: Flyline: a Bash plugin to replace readline for a modern line editing experience: syntax highlighting, agent integration, rich prompts, tooltips, fuzzy history search, and more!

commit.message:
Suggest prefixed buffer in agent error mode when no default agent command is configured (#410)

When a user presses Alt+Enter but only has prefix-triggered agent
commands configured (no default/`None`-keyed command), the error was a
generic "not configured" message with a prompt to run `--help`. Instead,
the error should recognize the existing prefix config, show the prefixed
buffer, and let Enter launch agent mode correctly.

## Changes

- **`ContentMode::AgentError`** — added `suggested_buffer:
Option<String>` to carry the user's buffer with a trigger prefix
prepended
- **`show_agent_mode_not_configured_error()`** — detects prefix-only
configs; picks the lexicographically smallest prefix for determinism,
builds `suggested_buffer = "<prefix><current_buffer>"`, and sets a
targeted error message
- **Enter handler for `AgentError`** — splits into two branches:
- `Some(buf)` → sets buffer to the suggested value and launches agent
mode via the normal prefix-matching path
  - `None` → existing behavior (submit `flyline agent-mode --help`)
- **Error display** — when `suggested_buffer` is set, shows the prefixed
buffer and `"Press Enter to launch agent mode with this buffer."`
instead of the raw-output/help UI

**Example scenario:** user has `--trigger-prefix "ai: "` but no default
command, types `find large files`, presses Alt+Enter:

```
No default agent mode configured, but you have agent mode configured with trigger prefix "ai: ".
Buffer with prefix: ai: find large files
Press Enter to launch agent mode with this buffer.
```

Pressing Enter then correctly launches agent mode with `"ai: find large
files"`, which matches the prefix, strips it, and sends `"find large
files"` to the configured command.

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: HalFrgrd <4559349+HalFrgrd@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
[BMC] Move platform_tests skip marks to correct conditional mark file (#23523)

Summary: Move 11 BMC-related platform_tests/ skip entries from tests_mark_conditions.yaml to tests_mark_conditions_platform_tests.yaml to avoid duplicate key conflicts.

When BMC skip marks for platform_tests/ paths are defined in the main tests_mark_conditions.yaml, they can conflict with entries in tests_mark_conditions_platform_tests.yaml that define conditions for the same test paths (e.g., platform_tests/broadcom/test_ser.py). This PR moves all BMC platform_tests skip marks to the correct file.

Signed-off-by: Jing Kan <jika@microsoft.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add WithMeterFactory source generator for IMeterFactory resolution (#459)

* Add WithMeterFactory source generator for IMeterFactory resolution

Add a new source generator that detects System.Diagnostics.DiagnosticSource
(version 10.0.0+) and generates a WithMeterFactory extension method on
AutoMocker. The extension registers a resolver for IMeterFactory with a
private implementation that forwards Create(MeterOptions) to new Meter(options).

The resolver is inserted after CacheResolver, following the established
pattern from other source generators (FakeLogging, KeyedServices, Options).

New files:
- MeterFactoryExtensionSourceGenerator.cs: The incremental source generator
- MeterFactoryGeneratorTests.cs: Four standard test cases

Modified files:
- CSharpSourceGeneratorVerifier.cs: Added ReferenceDiagnosticSource property
- Directory.Packages.props: Added System.Diagnostics.DiagnosticSource version
- Moq.AutoMock.props: Added EnableMoqAutoMockerMeterFactoryGenerator property

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add documentation for MeterFactory extension generator

Add MeterFactoryExtensionGenerator.md with usage, advanced scenarios,
and troubleshooting. Update SourceGenerators.md overview with section 6
and add to the disabling generators table.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Use InsertResolverAfter<CacheResolver> instead of manual loop

Replace the manual CacheResolver search + insert logic with the existing
InsertResolverAfter<CacheResolver> extension method.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add examples and tests for WithMeterFactory source generator

Includes a sample controller and unit tests across MSTest, NUnit, TUnit, and xUnit to demonstrate the WithMeterFactory extension. This also fixes namespaces in existing generator tests and simplifies several lambda expressions for consistency.

commit.message:
[bgp/agg]: Add BGP aggregate address test cases for Dual-Stack Concurrent Operation (#23547)

Add Test Group 9 covering dual-stack (IPv4 + IPv6) aggregate-address
concurrent operation:

- TC 9.1 test_dual_stack_simultaneous_aggregates: Both IPv4 and IPv6
  aggregates configured and active simultaneously via a single GCU patch
- TC 9.2 test_dual_stack_bbr_toggle: Both address families with
  bbr-required=true respond correctly to BBR state changes
- TC 9.3 test_dual_stack_mixed_bbr_required: Mixed bbr-required flags
  across address families — only the bbr-required aggregate is withdrawn
  when BBR is disabled

Route injection uses ExaBGP from M0 downstream neighbors.  Aggregate
route verification polls the DUT BGP table via vtysh.  All verify helpers
use wait_until polling to handle bgpcfgd processing delays.

Signed-off-by: Shixi Zhang <shixz@microsoft.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Added cooksAPI for location-based queries
- Updated cookAPI with profile/delivery-zone endpoints
- Menu fetched from API based on location

repository.name: homebites
repository.description: HomeBites - Home Cook Food Delivery Platform built with React, Redux Toolkit & Tailwind CSS

commit.message:
feat: Add Next.js frontend for Project Posting, Bidding, and Management System

- Add package.json, tsconfig.json, and next.config.js for Next.js 14 client
- Add services/projects.ts with all types/enums and API fetch helpers
- Add services/bids.ts for bid-specific API calls
- Add hooks: useProjects, useBids, useKanban with optimistic updates
- Add 9 components: ProjectCard, ProjectFilters, BidCard, BidForm,
  MilestoneList, MilestoneForm, KanbanBoard, TaskCard, TaskForm
- Add 4 pages: browse (index), detail ([id]), create (multi-step), my dashboard
- Use inline styles only; no external CSS frameworks

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
